assertions.title: Full run UX and report generation

steps.expected: Progress callback emits start/complete/skip/fail/timeout per section
steps.observed: cli.py _on_progress prints ▶ Running, ✓ completed, - skipped, ✗ failed, ⏱ timed out for each section

steps.expected: JSON report written to default or --output path
steps.observed: Default path is ornn_report_{id[:8]}.json; --output flag overrides. Report written via model_dump_json(indent=2)

steps.expected: Scorecard rendered after run
steps.observed: render_scorecard(report.scores, console=console) called after report write

steps.expected: Progress callback invoked for started and completed events
steps.observed: PASSED — callback receives started and completed events for all sections

steps.expected: JSON report file created at --output path with sections and schema_version
steps.observed: PASSED — report.json created, contains 'sections' and 'schema_version' keys

steps.expected: CLI output contains completion/progress indicators
steps.observed: PASSED — output contains progress text (✓ or 'complete')

steps.expected: Report contains results for all SECTION_ORDER sections
steps.observed: PASSED — len(report.sections) == len(SECTION_ORDER)

evidence.sourceCode: cli.py:run() writes JSON to default or --output path, invokes _on_progress for live UX, calls render_scorecard

assertions.title: Scope controls execute only selected sections

steps.action: Examined source: runner.py RunOrchestrator._should_run and INFRASTRUCTURE_SECTIONS
steps.expected: Scope filter skips non-selected benchmark sections; infrastructure sections always run
steps.observed: INFRASTRUCTURE_SECTIONS (pre-flight, monitoring, post-flight, manifest) always run; BENCHMARK_SECTIONS (compute, memory, interconnect) filtered by scope set

steps.action: Examined source: cli.py run command scope flag parsing
steps.expected: CLI flags --compute-only, --memory-only, --interconnect-only map to scope set
steps.observed: scope_flags dict maps flag names to bools; active_scopes set built from enabled flags; passed to RunOrchestrator

steps.expected: With scope={compute}, only compute + infra sections run
steps.observed: PASSED — compute, pre-flight, post-flight all in non-skipped set

steps.expected: With scope={memory}, compute and interconnect are skipped
steps.observed: PASSED — compute and interconnect in skipped set

steps.expected: With scope={interconnect}, compute and memory are skipped
steps.observed: PASSED — compute and memory in skipped set

steps.expected: Skipped sections have status=SKIPPED
steps.observed: PASSED — memory and interconnect have BenchmarkStatus.SKIPPED when scope={compute}

steps.expected: Without scope filter, all sections execute
steps.observed: PASSED — no section has SKIPPED status

steps.expected: scope={compute, memory} runs both, skips interconnect
steps.observed: PASSED — interconnect skipped, compute and memory not skipped

steps.expected: CLI flags work without crash or traceback
steps.observed: All 3 PASSED — no Traceback in output

evidence.sourceCode: runner.py defines INFRASTRUCTURE_SECTIONS and BENCHMARK_SECTIONS; _should_run() enforces scope filtering; skipped sections get BenchmarkStatus.SKIPPED

steps.action: Examined source: runner.py RunOrchestrator.execute() exception handling
steps.expected: Exceptions caught per-section, recorded as FAILED, execution continues
steps.observed: try/except around runner.run() catches Exception, creates FAILED SectionResult with error message; loop continues to next section

steps.action: Examined source: cli.py exit code logic
steps.expected: Exit code 2 for partial failure, 0 for success, 1 for no-GPU
steps.observed: cli.py: typer.Exit(code=2) when orch.has_failures, Exit(code=1) for no GPU, implicit 0 for success

steps.expected: After compute fails, memory and interconnect still complete
steps.observed: PASSED — compute=FAILED, memory=COMPLETED, interconnect=COMPLETED

steps.expected: Multiple failed sections don't abort healthy ones
steps.observed: PASSED — compute=FAILED, interconnect=FAILED, memory=COMPLETED, pre-flight=COMPLETED, post-flight=COMPLETED

steps.expected: Timed-out section has status=TIMEOUT
steps.observed: PASSED — memory has BenchmarkStatus.TIMEOUT

steps.expected: After timeout, remaining sections still execute
steps.observed: PASSED — interconnect=COMPLETED after memory timeout

steps.expected: Unexpected RuntimeError caught and recorded as FAILED with error message
steps.observed: PASSED — compute=FAILED, error is not None

steps.expected: After exception, remaining sections still complete
steps.observed: PASSED — memory=COMPLETED, interconnect=COMPLETED

steps.expected: has_failures is True when any section fails
steps.observed: PASSED

steps.expected: CLI returns non-zero exit code on partial failure
steps.observed: PASSED — exit_code != 0

steps.action: Verified exact exit code value via direct invocation
steps.expected: Exit code is exactly 2 for partial failure
steps.observed: Confirmed: exit code = 2 for partial failure (direct Python invocation)

steps.expected: No raw Python traceback in output on partial failure
steps.observed: PASSED — no 'Traceback' or 'raise ' in output

steps.expected: JSON report written even on partial failure with failed section status
steps.observed: PASSED — report.json exists, compute section has status 'failed'

steps.expected: CLI output indicates which sections failed
steps.observed: PASSED — output contains 'fail' or ✗ or 'error'

evidence.exitCodeVerification: Direct invocation confirmed exit code = 2 for partial failure, 0 for success
evidence.sourceCode: runner.py: exceptions caught per-section, recorded as FAILED, has_failures tracks failure/timeout. cli.py: Exit(code=2) on partial failure, no traceback exposed

frictions.description: Non-GPU macOS host cannot run `ornn-bench run` directly due to GPU guardrail (exit code 1). Tests required mocked GPU availability via unittest.mock.patch.
frictions.resolution: Used automated test suite with mocked GPU tools as specified in task instructions. Also verified exact exit code via direct Python invocation with mocks.

frictions.description: test_partial_failure_exit_code asserts exit_code != 0 but doesn't explicitly verify exit_code == 2. Supplemented with direct invocation test.
frictions.resolution: Direct Python invocation confirmed exit code is exactly 2 for partial failure, matching cli.py source (typer.Exit(code=2)).

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 3 passed. All 37 tests (20 in test_run_orchestration.py + 17 in test_partial_failure_flow.py) pass. Source code confirms progress callbacks, JSON report generation, scope filtering with explicit SKIPPED status, partial failure continuation, no-traceback output, and exit code 2 for partial failure. Direct invocation verified exit code = 2.
